Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Observations from the log Case 1 RA VF AE RMI VF AD SC AC Case 2 RA VF AE AD SC AC Case 3 RA VF AE RMI RMI VF AD SC AC We note that The process starts with RA then VF then AE After AE sometimes we have RMI and then VF again and sometimes we do not After the optional loop of RMI and VF we have AD SC and AC in sequence

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We can break down the process as Step 1 Receive Application RA Step 2 Validate Form VF Step 3 Assess Eligibility AE

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: So the overall process is RA VF AE optionally RMI plus VF then AD SC AC

Name: Validation - PE
Text: But wait in case 2 we do not have the optional block so the optional block is RMI plus VF and it can occur zero or more times

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait in case 3 after the first RMI we have another RMI then VF So it is not a simple loop of RMI VF because then we would expect VF after each RMI

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This might indicate that the process allows multiple requests for missing info before a re validation

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: So maybe the process is after AE we have an optional block that consists of one or more RMI and then a VF

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: After AE we have two possibilities a go to AD if no missing info b Request missing info RMI and then re validate VF and then possibly again until we are done then go to AD

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: We can model the multiple RMI as loop RMI tau Then after the loop which gives us one or more RMI we have a VF So the missing info subprocess is sequence loop RMI tau then VF

Name: Validation - PE
Text: In case 1 we have one RMI so the loop RMI tau would do RMI then exit the loop Then we do VF In case 3 we have two RMI the loop RMI tau would do RMI then tau then RMI again then exit So this models the multiple RMI without VF in between

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: After the choice we either have AD and then SC and AC or we have the missing info subprocess which ends with VF and then we have AD SC AC

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Notice that the sequence AD SC AC appears in both branches We can factor that out Let Tail be sequence AD then SC then AC

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So the final process tree structure is sequence RA then sequence VF then sequence AE then XOR sequence AD then sequence SC AC and sequence loop RMI tau then sequence VF then sequence AD then sequence SC AC

Name: Validation - PE
Text: This is the process tree that captures the observed behavior This model has Fitness It can replay all traces in the log Precision It does not allow too much extra behavior that is not in the log Simplicity It uses the basic operators and is not overly complex
